{{Short description|none}} {{Use British English|date=June 2021}} {{Use dmy dates|date=July 2021}} {{Infobox Film festival | name = 2021 Cannes Film Festival | image = 2021 Cannes Film Festival.jpg | caption = Official poster of the 74th Cannes Film Festival featuring Jury President [[Spike Lee]] | opening = ''[[Annette (film)|Annette]]'' | closing = ''[[OSS 117: From Africa with Love]]'' | location = [[Cannes]], [[France]] | founded = 1946 | awards = [[Palme d'Or]]:
''[[Titane (film)|Titane]]'' | host =[[Doria Tillier]] | number = 24 (In Competition) | date = 6–17 July 2021 | website = {{URL|festival-cannes.com/en}} | main = [[Cannes Film Festival]] | previous = [[2020 Cannes Film Festival|2020]] | next = [[2022 Cannes Film Festival|2022]] }} The '''74th annual [[Cannes Film Festival]]''' took place from 6 to 17 July 2021,{{cite web|url=https://www.festival-cannes.com/en/infos-communiques/communique/articles/presse-release|title=Presse release - Festival de Cannes|work=festival-cannes.com|date=27 January 2021|access-date=27 January 2021|archive-date=28 January 2021|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210128090741/https://www.festival-cannes.com/en/infos-communiques/communique/articles/presse-release|url-status=dead}} after having been originally scheduled from 11 to 22 May 2021.{{cite web|url=https://deadline.com/2020/10/cannes-film-festival-2020-opening-special-2021-dates-1234603994/|first=Nancy|last=Tartaglione|title=Cannes Film Festival Kicks Off Three-Day 'Spécial' Event Far From The Madding Crowd; Notes 2021 Contingency Plans|work=[[Deadline Hollywood|Deadline]]|date=27 October 2020|access-date=2 January 2021|archive-date=29 November 2020|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20201129143716/https://deadline.com/2020/10/cannes-film-festival-2020-opening-special-2021-dates-1234603994/|url-status=live}} American filmmaker [[Spike Lee]] was invited to be the president of the jury for the main competition for the festival, after the [[COVID-19 pandemic in France]] scuttled plans to have him head the jury of the [[2020 Cannes Film Festival]].{{cite news |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/entertainment-arts-56413896 |title=Cannes Film Festival: Spike Lee asked again to be first black jury head |work=BBC News |date=16 March 2021 |access-date=17 March 2021 |archive-date=11 November 2021 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20211111145313/https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/entertainment-arts-56413896 |url-status=live }} French actress [[Doria Tillier]] hosted the opening and closing ceremonies.{{cite news |date=16 July 2021 |title=Doria Tillier, maîtresse de cérémonie du 74e Festival de Cannes |url=https://www.france24.com/fr/vid%C3%A9o/20210716-doria-tillier-ma%C3%AEtresse-de-c%C3%A9r%C3%A9monie-du-74e-festival-de-cannes |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210729073732/https://www.france24.com/fr/vid%C3%A9o/20210716-doria-tillier-ma%C3%AEtresse-de-c%C3%A9r%C3%A9monie-du-74e-festival-de-cannes |archive-date=29 July 2021 |access-date=29 July 2021 |work=France 24 |language=fr}} French filmmaker [[Julia Ducournau]] won the ''[[Palme d'Or]]'' for the horror-drama film ''[[Titane]],'' becoming the second female director to ever win the award and the first to not win jointly with another filmmaker (at the [[1993 Cannes Film Festival|1993 edition]] [[Jane Campion]] had won jointly with [[Chen Kaige]]).{{cite news |date=17 July 2021 |title=Cannes Film Festival: Titane wins top Palme d'Or prize |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/entertainment-arts-57875578 |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210718005210/https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/entertainment-arts-57875578 |archive-date=18 July 2021 |access-date=18 July 2021 |work=BBC News}} The [[Honorary Palme d'Or]] was awarded to American actress and filmmaker [[Jodie Foster]] and Italian filmmaker [[Marco Bellocchio]].{{Cite web|date=2021-06-22|title=Marx Can Wait: a new film and an Honorary Palme d'or for Marco Bellocchio|url=https://www.festival-cannes.com/en/festival/actualites/articles/httpswww.festival-cannes.com/en/festival/actualites/articles/marx-can-wait-a-new-film-and-an-honorary-palme-dor-for-marco-bellocchio|access-date=2022-01-15|website=Festival de Cannes 2021|language=en}}{{Cite web|last1=Pulver|first1=Andrew|date=2 June 2021|title=Jodie Foster gets honorary Palme d'Or from Cannes film festival|url=https://www.theguardian.com/film/2021/jun/02/jodie-foster-gets-honorary-palme-dor-from-cannes-film-festival|website=The Guardian|language=en-UK|access-date=2 June 2021|archive-date=23 June 2021|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210623021804/https://www.theguardian.com/film/2021/jun/02/jodie-foster-gets-honorary-palme-dor-from-cannes-film-festival|url-status=live}} At the awards closing ceremony, on 17 July 2021, jury president Spike Lee made a gaffe by accidentally announcing the festival's top prize winner at the start of the night instead of the end.{{Cite web|last1=Cabrera|first1=Michael|last2=Rantala|first2=Hanna|date=17 July 2021|title=Spike Lee jumps gun at Cannes with 'Titane' Palme d'Or reveal|url=https://www.reuters.com/lifestyle/titane-by-frances-ducournau-wins-palme-dor-cannes-2021-07-17/|url-status=live|access-date=26 July 2021|website=Reuters|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210717184638/https://www.reuters.com/lifestyle/titane-by-frances-ducournau-wins-palme-dor-cannes-2021-07-17/ |archive-date=17 July 2021 }}{{Cite web|last=Lattanzio|first=Ryan|date=17 July 2021|title=Spike Lee Addresses Cannes Palme d'Or Slip-Up: 'I Have No Excuses, I Messed Up'|url=https://www.indiewire.com/2021/07/cannes-spike-lee-titane-palme-dor-1234651961/|access-date=26 July 2021|website=IndieWire|language=en|archive-date=19 July 2021|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210719215216/https://www.indiewire.com/2021/07/cannes-spike-lee-titane-palme-dor-1234651961/|url-status=live}} The festival opened with ''[[Annette (film)|Annette]]'' by [[Leos Carax]],{{Cite web|last1=Greater|first1=Tom|date=19 April 2021|title=Leos Carax's 'Annette' Starring Marion Cotillard & Adam Driver To Open Cannes Film Festival|url=https://deadline.com/2021/04/annette-marion-cotillard-adam-driver-open-cannes-film-festival-1234739250/|access-date=19 April 2021|website=Deadline|language=en-US|archive-date=23 April 2021|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210423235155/https://deadline.com/2021/04/annette-marion-cotillard-adam-driver-open-cannes-film-festival-1234739250/|url-status=live}} and closed with ''[[OSS 117: From Africa with Love]]'' by [[Nicolas Bedos]]. ==Juries== ===Main competition=== * [[Spike Lee]], American filmmaker - Jury President * [[Mati Diop]], French-Senegalese filmmaker and actress * [[Mylène Farmer]], French singer, songwriter and actress * [[Maggie Gyllenhaal]], American actress, filmmaker and producer * [[Jessica Hausner]], Austrian filmmaker * [[Mélanie Laurent]],
